goals.description: Hurry up and finish the last 2% of the assignment just enough to send it, then immediately leave for the cafeteria.
goals.rationale: My 'urgent_submit' deadline (Mon 13:30) has passed, creating a spike in stress. While I usually avoid conflict, the professor's message ('On my way!') triggered a fear of awkwardness. My impulsive nature wants to resolve this specific friction point *right now* so I can stop thinking about it and get to my friends. I'm not doing this for the grade; I'm doing it to clear the path to my high-value affiliation goal.

goals.description: Send the submission with a casual, slightly apologetic but breezy note to keep the professor happy without engaging in deep conflict.
goals.rationale: I need to manage the relationship with 'prof_assignment' to avoid future friction. A quick, non-confrontational submission satisfies my avoidant trait by smoothing over the delay, allowing me to mentally categorize the task as 'done' and shift focus entirely to my cohort_3 group.

goals.description: Leave the desk immediately after sending and join cohort_3 at the cafeteria to maintain social harmony.
goals.rationale: This is my core value driver (0.5). Once the immediate tension of the submission is resolved, my priority shifts instantly to the dopamine hit of being with my friends. I will ignore the sleep debt and any lingering anxiety about the grade loss because the present moment with my friends is the only thing that matters right now.
